sports-starter.team-sport.debreceni-civi.prompt: What baseball play records two outs during one continuous sequence?
sports-starter.team-sport.debreceni-civi.explanation: A double play records two outs on the same continuous play.
sports-starter.team-sport.debreceni-civi.details: A common version is the 6-4-3 double play: shortstop to second baseman to first baseman. Double plays often begin with a ground ball when a runner is forced to advance.

sports-starter.team-venue.cn-atletic-bar.prompt: Including the goalkeeper, how many players from each water polo team are in the pool during play?
sports-starter.team-venue.cn-atletic-bar.explanation: A water polo team fields seven players in the pool: six field players and one goalkeeper.
sports-starter.team-venue.cn-atletic-bar.details: Teams may substitute from the bench according to competition rules. Players tread water throughout the game because they may not stand on or push off the bottom of the pool.

sports-starter.athlete-sport.annegret-ri.prompt: How many track-and-field events make up a decathlon?
sports-starter.athlete-sport.annegret-ri.explanation: A decathlon combines ten track-and-field events over two days.
sports-starter.athlete-sport.annegret-ri.details: Athletes earn points from performances in sprints, distance running, hurdles, jumps, and throws. The winner is the competitor with the highest combined points total rather than the most individual event victories.

sports-starter.team-sport.fort-worth-fly.prompt: What basketball achievement records double-digit totals in three statistical categories in one game?
sports-starter.team-sport.fort-worth-fly.explanation: A triple-double means reaching at least ten in three major statistical categories in one game.
sports-starter.team-sport.fort-worth-fly.details: The categories are usually points, rebounds, assists, steals, or blocked shots. Points, rebounds, and assists are the most common combination.

sports-starter.athlete-sport.marcel-jacq.prompt: After a knockdown in boxing, what count must a fallen boxer beat to continue?
sports-starter.athlete-sport.marcel-jacq.explanation: A knocked-down boxer must rise before the referee reaches the count of ten.
sports-starter.athlete-sport.marcel-jacq.details: The referee counts while assessing whether the boxer can safely continue. Reaching a count of ten results in a knockout, while competition rules may also require an eight count before action resumes.

sports-starter.competition-sport.new-sou.prompt: What rugby infringement occurs when a player loses the ball forward from the hands?
sports-starter.competition-sport.new-sou.explanation: A knock-on occurs when the ball goes forward from a player's hand or arm and touches the ground or another player.
sports-starter.competition-sport.new-sou.details: The usual restart is a scrum awarded to the opposing team, although advantage may allow play to continue. The judgment depends on the ball moving toward the opponent's dead-ball line.

sports-starter.team-sport.comilla-victor.prompt: In cricket, what do the initials LBW stand for?
sports-starter.team-sport.comilla-victor.explanation: LBW stands for leg before wicket, one of cricket's methods of dismissing a batter.
sports-starter.team-sport.comilla-victor.details: An umpire considers where the ball pitched, where it struck the batter, whether a bat contact occurred, and whether it would have hit the wicket. The law prevents a batter from unfairly using the body to block a ball destined for the stumps.

sports-starter.athlete-sport.bim-diederi.prompt: Which cycling discipline sends riders over grass, mud, and obstacles on a short circuit?
sports-starter.athlete-sport.bim-diederi.explanation: Cyclocross is contested on short mixed-surface circuits that often include barriers and steep sections.
sports-starter.athlete-sport.bim-diederi.details: Riders may need to dismount, shoulder the bicycle, and run before remounting. Races are usually held in autumn and winter and reward handling skill as much as speed and endurance.

sports-starter.team-sport.kansas-city-co.prompt: How many points does a safety score in football?
sports-starter.team-sport.kansas-city-co.explanation: A safety awards two points to the defending team.
sports-starter.team-sport.kansas-city-co.details: A common safety occurs when the offense is responsible for the ball becoming dead in its own end zone. The team that conceded the safety must then put the ball back into play with a free kick.

sports-starter.team-sport.klm-line.prompt: The Soviet Union's famous KLM Line competed in which sport?
sports-starter.team-sport.klm-line.explanation: The KLM Line was a dominant Soviet ice hockey forward unit.
sports-starter.team-sport.klm-line.details: Its name came from the surnames of Vladimir Krutov, Igor Larionov, and Sergei Makarov. The trio starred internationally during the 1980s and later played in the NHL.

sports-starter.competition-sport.champ-c.prompt: What is the first starting spot on a motor-racing grid called?
sports-starter.competition-sport.champ-c.explanation: Pole position is the leading place on the starting grid.
sports-starter.competition-sport.champ-c.details: It is usually earned by setting the fastest qualifying time, although some series use additional qualifying races or other formats. The pole sitter begins with the clearest track and shortest route into the opening corner.

sports-starter.athlete-country.egon-mull.prompt: Which famous motorcycle race is held on public roads on the Isle of Man?
sports-starter.athlete-country.egon-mull.explanation: The Isle of Man TT is a motorcycle time trial run over closed public roads.
sports-starter.athlete-country.egon-mull.details: Competitors start individually and race against the clock around the long Snaefell Mountain Course. Its speed, changing terrain, and roadside hazards make it one of motorcycling's most demanding events.

sports-starter.competition-sport.speedwa.prompt: Competitors in the Speedway Grand Prix race what type of vehicle?
sports-starter.competition-sport.speedwa.explanation: The Speedway Grand Prix is the world championship series for motorcycle speedway.
sports-starter.competition-sport.speedwa.details: Riders race specialized motorcycles around short oval dirt tracks, sliding through corners at high speed. Traditional speedway machines have no brakes and use a single fixed gear.

sports-starter.athlete-country.helga-mee.prompt: Which U.S. city hosted the 1996 Summer Olympics?
sports-starter.athlete-country.helga-mee.explanation: Atlanta hosted the 1996 Summer Olympic Games.
sports-starter.athlete-country.helga-mee.details: The centennial Olympics marked one hundred years since the first modern Games in Athens. Muhammad Ali lit the cauldron during the opening ceremony.

sports-starter.athlete-country.magdalena.prompt: The Olympic motto Citius, Altius, Fortius translates to “Faster, Higher,” and what?
sports-starter.athlete-country.magdalena.explanation: Citius, Altius, Fortius means Faster, Higher, Stronger.
sports-starter.athlete-country.magdalena.details: The International Olympic Committee added the word Communiter, meaning Together, in 2021. The expanded motto emphasizes both individual excellence and solidarity.

sports-starter.athlete-country.anni-biec.prompt: What name is given to the three curved shapes in the Paralympic symbol?
sports-starter.athlete-country.anni-biec.explanation: The three elements of the Paralympic symbol are called Agitos.
sports-starter.athlete-country.anni-biec.details: Agito is Latin for “I move.” The red, blue, and green shapes encircle a central point and express the role of the Paralympic Movement in bringing athletes together from around the world.

sports-starter.team-venue.donetsk-people.prompt: In baseball statistics, what do the initials ERA stand for?
sports-starter.team-venue.donetsk-people.explanation: ERA stands for earned run average, a measure of runs charged to a pitcher.
sports-starter.team-venue.donetsk-people.details: It estimates how many earned runs a pitcher allows per nine innings. Runs resulting from certain fielding errors are unearned and do not count against the pitcher's ERA.

sports-starter.competition-sport.yugosla.prompt: How many players from one team are normally on the court in team handball?
sports-starter.competition-sport.yugosla.explanation: A handball team fields seven players: six court players and one goalkeeper.
sports-starter.competition-sport.yugosla.details: Substitutions are unlimited and can occur during play through the team's substitution area. Players advance the ball by passing, dribbling, or taking a limited number of steps while holding it.

sports-starter.athlete-sport.garry-kaspa.prompt: What chess result occurs when a player has no legal move but is not in check?
sports-starter.athlete-sport.garry-kaspa.explanation: Stalemate occurs when the player to move has no legal move and the king is not in check.
sports-starter.athlete-sport.garry-kaspa.details: A stalemate immediately ends the game as a draw. Checkmate differs because the king is under attack and no legal move can escape that attack.

sports-starter.team-venue.kolkata-knight.prompt: Which cricket fielder stands directly behind the wicket at the striker's end?
sports-starter.team-venue.kolkata-knight.explanation: The wicketkeeper fields behind the striker's wicket.
sports-starter.team-venue.kolkata-knight.details: The role combines catching, stopping missed deliveries, and attempting stumpings or run-outs. The wicketkeeper is the only fielder permitted to wear external leg guards and wicketkeeping gloves.

sports-starter.team-venue.dinamo-sokhumi.prompt: What ice hockey violation generally involves shooting the puck from behind center across the opponent's goal line untouched?
sports-starter.team-venue.dinamo-sokhumi.explanation: Icing is called when a team sends the puck across both the center red line and opposing goal line without a qualifying touch.
sports-starter.team-venue.dinamo-sokhumi.details: The rule discourages teams from simply firing the puck the length of the rink to escape pressure. Exceptions and hybrid-icing procedures vary by situation and league.

sports-starter.competition-sport.gauliga.prompt: What is it called when one soccer player scores three goals in a match?
sports-starter.competition-sport.gauliga.explanation: Three goals by one player in a soccer match are called a hat trick.
sports-starter.competition-sport.gauliga.details: The goals do not need to be consecutive unless the feat is described as a natural or perfect hat trick under a more specific convention. The term is also used in several other sports.

sports-starter.athlete-country.michael-u.prompt: What is the circular scoring target at each end of a curling sheet called?
sports-starter.athlete-country.michael-u.explanation: The concentric target rings in curling are called the house.
sports-starter.athlete-country.michael-u.details: Only stones in or touching the house can score. After all stones are delivered in an end, the team with the stone closest to the button scores one point for each stone closer than the opponent's nearest stone.
